nodes.statement_excerpt: For $ C=\{c\}$ the block polynomial is \[ G_{\{c\},c}(Y,Z)=(1+Y)^c+(Z-1)Y^c, \] and thm:exact-divisor-block-support-ledger gives \[ | S_c(A)| ={n/c}{ A/c} {n-c A/c}{A-c A/c}, \] the summand used in the safe ledger.

nodes.label: def:divisor-block-support-ledger-certificate

nodes.label: rem:support-vs-slope-ledger
nodes.statement_excerpt: thm:exact-divisor-block-support-ledger closes the divisor-lattice overlap problem at the support-family level: intersections between quotient descriptions at incomparable divisor scales are now counted by one coefficient extraction in the common $h$-block. This is still weaker than the desired distinct-slope quotient ledger. To finish that stronger ledger one must apply the exact support-image maps of prop:distinct-parameter-line-ledger,prop:distinct-parameter-curve-ledger to the support set counted here and then prove or certify the resulting parameter image size after duplicate coalescing.

nodes.label: lem:support-locator-syndrome-recurrence
nodes.statement_excerpt: Let $Y:D$ and let $T D$ with $|T|=j r$. Put $S=D T$ and $t=r-j$. Then $Y$ is explained by $C$ on $S$ if and only if \[ H_{t,j}((Y))_T=0 . \]

nodes.label: lem:exact-line-image-map

source.title: exact affine and projective support-image map

nodes.statement_excerpt: Let $f,g:D$, put $u=(f)$ and $v=(g)$, and fix $T D$ with $|T|=j r$ and $S=D T$. Define \[ A_T:=H_{t,j}(u)_T, B_T:=H_{t,j}(v)_T . \] A finite slope $z$ is explained on the fixed support $S$ and is support-wise noncontained there if and only if \[ A_T+zB_T=0, B_T0 . \] Consequently the fixed support contributes either no finite bad slope or the unique value \[ _T=-A_{T,m}/B_{T,m} \] for any coordinate $m$ with $B_{T,m}0$, provided all nonzero coordinates give the same value. For the projective line $ f+ g$, the fixed support contributes a support-wise noncontained point $[:] P^1()$ if and only if $A_T$ and $B_T$ are linearly dependent and not b

nodes.label: prop:distinct-parameter-line-ledger

Hence the exact quotient-branch line numerator is \[ |_{ C}^{ aff}(f,g;a)| U_{ C}^{ supp}(a). \] The corresponding projective-slope numerator is \[ |_{ C}^{ proj}(f,g;a)| U_{ C}^{ supp}(a). \] The same image ledgers upper-bound no-loss CA parameters whose explaining support lies in the same quotient family.

With the notation of lem:exact-curve-image-map, write \[ P_{T,m}() :=_{i=0}^d ^i (V_{i,T})_m, 0 m<t, t=|S|-k . \] Define the fixed-support contribution polynomial $g_T^{(d)}()$ as follows. For a degree-$d$ finite-parameter power curve define \[ R_{ T}^{(d)}() :=radlcm_{T T} g_T^{(d)}(), \] where factors equal to $1$ are ignored and the empty lcm is $1$. For the quotient branch at agreement threshold $a>k$ and divisor set $ C$, we use \[ T= T_{ C}^{ a} :=\{D S:S S_{ C}^{ a}\}. \] The finite-field root-count polynomial is \[ R_{ T,F}^{(d)}() :=(R_{ T}^{(d)}(),^q-). \] Because $R_{ T}^{(d)}$ is squarefree, the degree of $R_{ T,F}^{(d)}$ is the number of $$-rational roots of $R_{ T}^{(d)}$.

nodes.label: thm:exact-quotient-image-lcm-ledger
nodes.statement_excerpt: Let $a>k$, let $ C$ be a finite divisor set, and let $ T_{ C}^{ a}$ be the quotient co-support family. For every finite-parameter degree-$d$ power curve, the exact quotient-branch support-wise curve-MCA numerator is \[ { |_{ C}^{(d)}(f_0,,f_d;a)| = R_{ T_{ C}^{ a},F}^{(d)} . } \] Consequently \[ |_{ C}^{(d)}(f_0,,f_d;a)| R_{ T_{ C}^{ a}}^{(d)} d\,U_{ C}^{ supp}(a), \] and the final support count may be evaluated by the exact divisor-block formula of thm:exact-divisor-block-support-ledger. The same lcm ledger upper-bounds no-loss curve-CA parameters whose explaining support lies in the same quotient family.

nodes.statement_excerpt: For an affine received line $f+Zg$, take $d=1$, $f_0=f$, and $f_1=g$. Then $R_{ T_{ C}^{ a}}^{(1)}(Z)$ is the squarefree product of the linear factors $Z-z$ for the distinct finite quotient-witnessed support-wise MCA-bad slopes. Hence \[ \#\{{finite quotient-witnessed MCA-bad slopes}\} = R_{ T_{ C}^{ a}}^{(1)} . \] The same polynomial upper-bounds no-loss CA finite slopes whose explaining support lies in the declared quotient family.

nodes.label: def:projective-line-lcm-ledger

Otherwise let $h_T^{ proj}$ be the gcd of the nonzero linear forms, normalized by a fixed monomial order. Thus $h_T^{ proj}$ is either $1$ or a homogeneous linear form. For a co-support family $ T$ define \[ R_{ T}^{ proj}(Z_0,Z_1) :=radlcm_{T T}h_T^{ proj}. \]

nodes.label: cor:projective-line-lcm-ledger
nodes.statement_excerpt: For projective slopes, the exact quotient-branch support-wise MCA numerator is \[ R_{ T_{ C}^{ a}}^{ proj}. \] The same degree upper-bounds no-loss projective-CA parameters whose explaining support lies in the quotient family.

nodes.label: rem:lcm-image-ledger-status

source.title: status after the lcm image ledger

nodes.statement_excerpt: The quotient ledgers now separate three tasks that were previously entangled. First, thm:quotient-remainder-deep-floor supplies lower floors by heaviest generated-field prefix fibers. Second, thm:exact-divisor-block-support-ledger counts the declared quotient support union with divisor overlaps handled exactly. Third, thm:exact-quotient-image-lcm-ledger,cor:affine-line-lcm-ledger,cor:projective-line-lcm-ledger push that support family through the fixed-locator equations and coalesce duplicate finite, projective, and curve parameters by gcd/lcm arithmetic. The remaining quotient-side obstruction is not duplicate coalescing inside a declared qu

nodes.label: def:quotient-image-certificate
nodes.statement_excerpt: A quotient image certificate for a line or curve ledger consists of: {enumerate}[label=()] the divisor set $ C$ and the agreement threshold $a$; a deterministic description, hash, or orbit decomposition of $ T_{ C}^{ a}$; for every represented co-support $T$, the locator coefficients $_T$ and the syndrome-window vectors $A_T,B_T$ for a line, or $V_{0,T},,V_{d,T}$ for a curve; the declared parameter value, parameter bucket, or the declaration that no parameter is contributed; a bucket table proving the number of distinct parameters after duplicate coalescing. {enumerate} The verifier checks the locator split condition, the Hankel equations, th

nodes.label: rem:distinct-ledger-status
nodes.statement_excerpt: The propositions above are exact for the declared quotient branch and are a real refinement over the support ledger: the numerator is an image size, not a support count. They still do not prove that all bad parameters are quotient-periodic. The remaining safe-side theorem for the full prize is the aperiodic Hankel-packing bound: after tangent and quotient image branches are removed, one must bound the parameters coming from co-supports outside the declared quotient families.

nodes.label: def:hankel-regularity-certificate
nodes.statement_excerpt: A regular Hankel minor certificate at exact agreement $A$ is a row set $I_A\{0,,t_A-1\}$ of size $j_A+1$ such that \[ _A(Z) := M_A(Z)_{I_A,\{0,,j_A\}} \] is not the zero polynomial in $F[Z]$. If no such row set exists, the line is called Hankel-singular at $A$.

nodes.label: lem:regular-exact-agreement-eliminant
nodes.statement_excerpt: Assume $t_A j_A+1$, and suppose $_A(Z)$ is a regular Hankel minor certificate. Every finite support-wise MCA-bad slope with a witness support of exact size $A$ is a root of $_A$. Hence the number of such slopes is at most \[ _A j_A+1=n-A+1 . \] The same root containment holds for no-loss CA-bad slopes with exact agreement $A$.

nodes.label: thm:regular-closed-ball-hankel-packing
nodes.statement_excerpt: Let $a k$ and suppose that for each exact agreement $A\{a,a+1,,n\}$ with $t_A j_A+1$ a regular Hankel minor certificate $_A$ is supplied. Then all bad finite slopes whose exact witness size is in this regular bucket lie in the root set of \[ _{ a}(Z):=_{A=a}^{n}_A(Z), \] with factors omitted for exact agreements outside the overdetermined range. Consequently their number is at most \[ _A _A . \] If exact root sets are supplied, roots already charged to the tangent or quotient-image ledgers may be removed from this count by duplicate coalescing.

nodes.label: def:canonical-affine-rankdrop-gcd
nodes.statement_excerpt: Fix an affine received line $f+Zg$, put $u=(f)$ and $v=(g)$, and assume $t_A j_A+1$. Set \[ M_A(Z):=H_{t_A,j_A}(u)+Z H_{t_A,j_A}(v). \] Let $ M_A(Z)$ be the finite set of all $(j_A+1)(j_A+1)$ maximal minors of $M_A(Z)$. If every element of $ M_A(Z)$ is the zero polynomial, the exact-agreement bucket is called rank-drop singular. Otherwise define \[ G_A^{ aff}(Z) :=rad\{(Z): M_A(Z),\ 0\}, \] with the gcd normalized to be monic. Over the finite field $$ of size $q$ set \[ G_{A,F}^{ aff}(Z):= (G_A^{ aff}(Z),Z^q-Z). \] Thus $G_{A,F}^{ aff}$ is the squarefree polynomial whose roots are precisely those $F$-rational parameters at which all nonzero m

nodes.label: thm:canonical-affine-rankdrop-ledger
nodes.statement_excerpt: Assume $t_A j_A+1$ and the exact-agreement bucket is not rank-drop singular. Every finite support-wise MCA-bad slope with an exact witness support of size $A$ is a root of $G_A^{ aff}$. Consequently the number of such slopes is at most \[ G_{A,F}^{ aff} G_A^{ aff} j_A+1=n-A+1 . \] The same bound holds for no-loss CA-bad slopes with exact agreement $A$.

nodes.label: rem:gcd-rankdrop-improves-one-minor

source.title: why this improves the one-minor certificate

nodes.statement_excerpt: lem:regular-exact-agreement-eliminant used one certified nonzero maximal minor and therefore counted the roots of a single determinant. The polynomial $G_A^{ aff}$ uses all maximal minors and counts their common finite-field root set. It is never worse than any one-minor certificate and can be much smaller; it is also canonical, so two scanners that use the same syndrome convention print the same regular-bucket polynomial.

nodes.label: def:closed-ball-rankdrop-lcm
nodes.statement_excerpt: For an agreement threshold $a>k$, let \[ A_{ reg}(a):= \{A\{a,a+1,,n\}: t_A j_A+1 { and the bucket }A{ is not rank-drop singular}\}. \] Define \[ G_{ a}^{ aff}(Z) :=radlcm_{A A_{ reg}(a)} G_A^{ aff}(Z), G_{ a,F}^{ aff}(Z):= (G_{ a}^{ aff}(Z),Z^q-Z), \] with the empty lcm equal to $1$. Exact agreement values $A\{a,,n\} A_{ reg}(a)$ are recorded as residual buckets: they are either underdetermined ($t_A<j_A+1$) or rank-drop singular.

nodes.label: cor:canonical-regular-closed-ball-hankel-packing
nodes.statement_excerpt: All finite support-wise MCA-bad slopes whose exact witness size lies in $ A_{ reg}(a)$ are roots of $G_{ a}^{ aff}$. Hence their number is at most \[ G_{ a,F}^{ aff} _{A A_{ reg}(a)}(n-A+1). \] The same bound holds for no-loss CA-bad slopes in the same regular buckets.

nodes.label: def:paid-root-removal-regular-branch

source.title: paid-root removal for the regular branch

nodes.statement_excerpt: Suppose a squarefree polynomial $P_{ paid,F}(Z)$ is supplied whose roots are exactly the finite-field parameters already charged to tangent/common-line and quotient-image ledgers. Define the regular residual polynomial \[ G_{{ reg}{ paid},F}^{ aff}(Z) :={G_{ a,F}^{ aff}(Z)} {(G_{ a,F}^{ aff}(Z),P_{ paid,F}(Z))} . \] Its degree is the number of regular rank-drop parameters not already paid by the previous ledgers.

nodes.label: cor:quotient-tangent-removed-regular-numerator
nodes.statement_excerpt: If $P_{ paid,F}$ is exact in the sense of def:paid-root-removal-regular-branch, then the number of finite slopes in the regular overdetermined branch that remain after removing the paid quotient/tangent parameters is at most \[ G_{{ reg}{ paid},F}^{ aff} . \] This is a safe aperiodic numerator for the regular buckets. The underdetermined and rank-drop singular exact-agreement buckets must still be handled by pivot charts or by a separately named residual obstruction.

nodes.label: def:curve-rankdrop-gcd
nodes.statement_excerpt: For a finite-parameter degree-$d$ curve \[ W_=f_0+ f_1++^d f_d, \] put $u_i=(f_i)$ and, at exact agreement $A$, define \[ M_A^{(d)}():= _{i=0}^{d}^i H_{t_A,j_A}(u_i). \] When $t_A j_A+1$ and not all maximal minors of $M_A^{(d)}$ vanish identically, set \[ G_A^{(d)}() :=rad\{(): { is a nonzero maximal minor of }M_A^{(d)}\}, \] and \[ G_{A,F}^{(d)}():= (G_A^{(d)}(),^q-). \]

nodes.label: thm:canonical-curve-rankdrop-ledger
nodes.statement_excerpt: In a non-singular overdetermined exact-agreement bucket, every finite curve-MCA-bad parameter is a root of $G_A^{(d)}$. Consequently the number of such parameters is at most \[ G_{A,F}^{(d)} G_A^{(d)} d(j_A+1). \] The same bound holds for no-loss curve-CA parameters in the same bucket. The closed-ball form is obtained by taking the squarefree lcm of the polynomials $G_A^{(d)}$ over the regular exact-agreement buckets and then taking the gcd with $^q-$.

nodes.label: def:regular-rankdrop-certificate
nodes.statement_excerpt: A regular rank-drop certificate for a row, a received affine line or finite curve, and an agreement threshold $a$ consists of: {enumerate}[label=(),leftmargin=2.2em] the syndrome vectors of the coefficient words and the exact-agreement range $A\{a,,n\}$; for every overdetermined exact agreement $A$, either a declaration that all maximal minors vanish identically, or the canonical gcd polynomial $G_A^{ aff}$, respectively $G_A^{(d)}$, together with ideal-membership or raw minor arithmetic proving that it is the gcd of all nonzero maximal minors; the squarefree lcm over all non-singular regular buckets and the finite-root factor obtained by gcd

nodes.label: rem:rankdrop-ledger-status
nodes.statement_excerpt: The regular overdetermined part of the aperiodic Hankel problem is now canonical: one no longer has to choose a minor or accept a degree sum when the common root set of all maximal minors is smaller. This still does not solve the near-capacity underdetermined buckets, which form the hard aperiodic residual class. Those buckets remain assigned to the affine pivot charts, projective-infinity charts, curve coefficient pivots, or named singular residual obstructions.

nodes.label: def:aperiodic-locator-chart
nodes.statement_excerpt: An aperiodic locator chart at exact agreement $A$ is a pair \[ X=(E_X,_X), \] where $E_X$ is a finite list of polynomial equations in the locator coefficients $$, and $_X()$ is a product of declared nonzero tests. The chart represents the constructible set \[ V(I_{ split,j}+ E_X) V(_X). \] The equations and inequations must include, or reference, the removal of already paid tangent/common-code-line and quotient-image branches. A family of charts $ X_A$ is a valid aperiodic chart cover if the union of their constructible sets contains every remaining split locator of exact co-support size $j_A$ not assigned to those earlier ledgers.

nodes.label: thm:aperiodic-affine-eliminant
nodes.statement_excerpt: Let $X$ be an aperiodic locator chart and let $h$ be a noncontainment pivot. If there is a nonzero polynomial \[ Q_{X,h}(Z) J^{ aff}_{X,h} F[Z] \] of degree $D_{X,h}$, then the number of finite support-wise MCA-bad slopes in that chart with $B_h0$ is at most $\{|F|,D_{X,h}\}$. The same bound holds for no-loss CA-bad slopes in that pivot chart.

nodes.label: rem:pivot-split-useful

source.title: why the pivot split is useful

nodes.statement_excerpt: The saturation by the whole vector $B$ in the unsplit incidence ideal is replaced by the explicit open cover $B_h0$. A scanner can try all $h$, produce small univariate eliminants on successful pivots, and report the residual locus $B_0==B_{t-1}=0$ as contained for support-wise MCA. For no-loss CA, that residual locus also contributes no bad slope at the same support, because if $A=B=0$ then both $f$ and $g$ are explained on the witness support.

nodes.label: def:projective-infinity-chart
nodes.statement_excerpt: For a chart $X$, the projective-infinity chart is the constructible locus \[ I_{ split,j}+ E_X+ B_0,,B_{t-1}, _X0, A0 . \] If it is nonempty, it contributes the single projective parameter $[0:1]$. If it is empty, the infinity point contributes nothing on $X$.

nodes.label: cor:projective-eliminant-certificate
nodes.statement_excerpt: For a chart cover $ X_A$, the projective-slope numerator is bounded by the union of the finite-patch root sets from thm:aperiodic-affine-eliminant together with at most one extra point $[0:1]$, provided the infinity charts are certified either empty or nonempty. Equivalently, a homogeneous nonzero eliminant \[ Q_X(Z_0,Z_1) \] of degree $D_X$ bounds the number of projective bad parameters in that chart by $D_X$.

nodes.label: thm:aperiodic-curve-eliminant
nodes.statement_excerpt: If a chart $X$ and coefficient pivot $(i,h)$ have a nonzero eliminant \[ Q_{X,i,h}() J^{ curve,d}_{X,i,h} F[] \] of degree $D_{X,i,h}$, then the number of finite degree-$d$ curve-MCA bad parameters in that chart and pivot is at most $\{|F|,D_{X,i,h}\}$. The same bound applies to no-loss curve-CA parameters in the same pivot chart.

nodes.label: thm:residual-rank-pivot-cover
nodes.statement_excerpt: Fix an exact agreement value $A$ and a chart cover $ X_A$ of the remaining locator branch after tangent/common-line and quotient-image removals. Every projective support-wise MCA-bad parameter with exact witness size $A$ in that remaining branch lies on one of the rank-pivot charts \[ J^{ proj}_{X,I,J,} \] for some $X X_A$, some rank $s$, some nonzero $s s$ minor $_{I,J}$ at the parameter, and some nonzero noncontainment coordinate $_$. The same cover contains every no-loss projective CA-bad parameter in that branch.

nodes.label: thm:projective-residual-eliminant-certificate
nodes.statement_excerpt: If a rank-pivot chart has a nonzero homogeneous eliminant \[ Q_{X,I,J,}(Z_0,Z_1) J^{ proj}_{X,I,J,} F[Z_0,Z_1] \] of degree $D_{X,I,J,}$, then the number of projective line parameters in that chart is at most $D_{X,I,J,}$. More exactly, over a finite field $F$ of size $q$, the number of finite-patch roots is \[ (Q_{X,I,J,}(1,Z),Z^q-Z), \] and the point at infinity contributes precisely when $Q_{X,I,J,}(0,1)=0$. Thus the exact finite-field count is \[ (Q_{X,I,J,}(1,Z),Z^q-Z) + 1_{Q_{X,I,J,}(0,1)=0}. \]

nodes.label: cor:finite-infinity-rank-pivot-patches
nodes.statement_excerpt: The finite affine rank-pivot certificate is obtained from thm:projective-residual-eliminant-certificate by setting $Z_0=1$ and using only noncontainment pivots $B_h0$. The infinity chart is obtained by setting $Z_0=0$, $Z_1=1$, imposing \[ B()=0, A_h()0 \] for some $h$, and applying the same saturated rank-pivot test to the constant matrix $V$. Consequently the projective sampler has no hidden extra case: every finite or infinite bad point is represented by one of these two patch types.

nodes.label: thm:curve-residual-rank-pivot-eliminant
nodes.statement_excerpt: The curve-bad parameters in a residual exact-agreement branch are covered by the charts $ J^{ curve,d}_{X,I,J,i,h}$. If such a chart has a nonzero eliminant \[ Q_{X,I,J,i,h}() J^{ curve,d}_{X,I,J,i,h} F[] \] of degree $D_{X,I,J,i,h}$, then that chart contributes at most \[ (Q_{X,I,J,i,h}(),^q-) D_{X,I,J,i,h} \] finite parameters over $F$. The same bound applies to no-loss curve-CA parameters in the same chart.

Then the number of extension-field affine parameters contributed by this chart is at most \[ _Y ||^{e_Y}. \] If $Y_Z$ is zero-dimensional and radical, this bound may be replaced by the exact number of its $$-rational points. After normalization by the line field, this chart contributes at most \[ {_Y ||^{e_Y}}{|F|} =_Y ||^{e_Y-m} \] to the MCA or CA error numerator.

nodes.statement_excerpt: Assume that the chart families $ R_A$, for $A a$, cover every remaining locator after tangent/common-line and quotient-image removals. Suppose each residual chart $Y$ is certified by one of the following data: {enumerate}[label=(),leftmargin=2.2em] an affine eliminant $Q_Y(Z)$, contributing $(Q_Y,Z^q-Z)$; a homogeneous projective eliminant $Q_Y(Z_0,Z_1)$, contributing $(Q_Y(1,Z),Z^q-Z)+ 1_{Q_Y(0,1)=0}$; a finite curve eliminant $Q_Y()$, contributing $(Q_Y,^q-)$; an extension Weil-restricted projection certificate $(_Y,e_Y)$, contributing $_Y||^{e_Y}$ parameters. {enumerate} Then the finite affine aperiodic numerator after quotient/tangent rem

nodes.label: def:singular-residual-certificate
nodes.statement_excerpt: A singular or underdetermined residual aperiodic certificate consists of: {enumerate}[label=(),leftmargin=2.2em] the paid tangent/common-line and quotient-image root ledgers already removed; the regular rank-drop gcd/lcm ledger and its paid-root removal; for each remaining exact agreement $A$, a chart cover of the residual locator set after the paid branches are removed; for every chart, its rank $s$, pivot minor $_{I,J}$, noncontainment pivot, and either an affine, projective, or curve eliminant, or a Weil-restricted projection degree/dimension certificate; a residual table for every chart on which no eliminant or projection bound is supplie

nodes.label: rem:singular-bucket-chart-status
nodes.statement_excerpt: This does not prove that the hard aperiodic residual buckets are small. It does remove one ambiguity: underdetermined and rank-drop singular buckets now have a precise finite certificate language, including finite slopes, projective infinity, finite-parameter curves, and genuinely extension-valued parameters. The next mathematical step is to construct low-degree eliminants, or prove structural classification, for these rank-pivot charts in the near-capacity regimes where $t_A=A-k$ is small.

nodes.label: rem:capfpr-weil-barrier

source.title: Weil barrier and the reason to measure joint cancellation

nodes.statement_excerpt: The per-power-sum Weil bound is the wrong endpoint for (Q). It pays a visible $ p$ cost per active power-sum direction, and the fable base-case theorem thm:capff1-collision-base shows exactly how far that method reaches at head depths. The computations described in rem:capff1-calibration indicate that the second-moment refinement over a full period returns the same square-root scale: individual character maxima are controlled by the wrong statistic. The normalized recursion should therefore be used to measure joint tails and collision gaps, not to claim a uniform character bound strong enough to imply (Q) by itself.

nodes.statement_excerpt: thm:capfp-Q-unify states that (Q) is the boundary profile $d_1=w'+1$ of the split pencil and the aperiodic balanced core consists of interior profiles; but prob:capfp-split,prob:capfr1-split-pencil quantify over ``balanced profile $d_1 w'+1$'', which includes the boundary and hence includes (Q) itself with the wrong ($q$-scale) model. Part (a) above identifies the boundary contribution. The intended reading --- boundary delegated to (Q), split-pencil census over $d_1 w'+2$ --- is adopted in the restatement below; part (b) shows that the interior model must also be base-field-normalized.
